nodes.title: Loop space
nodes.statement:
For a prestack $Z \in \PreStk$, the \emph{loop space} $\mathfrak{L}Z$
  is the prestack defined on $\AffSch$ by
  \[
    \Spec(A) \;\mapsto\; \Hom_{\AffSch}(\Spec(A((t))),\, Z).
  \]
  For $Z = \BA^1$ this gives $\LLA(\Spec A) = A((t))$, so $\LLA$ is the
  ind-scheme whose $A$-points are formal Laurent series with $A$-coefficients.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S2.1.1.}

nodes.title: Positive loop space
nodes.statement:
The \emph{positive loop space} $\mathfrak{L}^+ Z$ for $Z \in \PreStk$
  is defined by
  \[
    \Spec(A) \;\mapsto\; \Hom_{\AffSch}(\Spec(A[[t]]),\, Z).
  \]
  For $Z = \BA^1$ this gives $\LLAp(\Spec A) = A[[t]]$, so $\LLAp$ is the
  pro-scheme whose $A$-points are formal power series with $A$-coefficients.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S2.1.1.}

nodes.title: Truncated positive loop space
nodes.statement:
For $n \geq 0$ and $Z \in \PreStk$, the \emph{truncated positive loop space}
  $\LLAn{n} := \mathfrak{L}_n^+ Z$ is defined by
  \[
    \Spec(A) \;\mapsto\; \Hom_{\AffSch}(\Spec(A[[t]]/t^n),\, Z).
  \]
  For $Z = \BA^1$ this gives $\LLAn{n} \cong \BA^n$ via the identification
  $A[[t]]/t^n \cong A^n$ (coefficients $a_0,\ldots,a_{n-1}$).

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S2.1.1.}

nodes.title: Local systems on the punctured formal disc
nodes.statement:
The moduli stack of \emph{rank 1 de Rham local systems on the punctured
  formal disc} is the algebraic stack quotient
  \[
    \LocSysGm \;:=\; \LLAdt \,/\, \LLGm
  \]
  where $\LLAdt$ denotes the loop space of the torsor of $1$-forms $\BA^1 dt$,
  and $\LLGm$ acts by gauge transformations:
  $g \cdot \omega = g\omega + g^{-1}dg$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S2.3.2.}

nodes.title: Polar forms
nodes.statement:
A \emph{polar form} is an element of $\LLAdt / \LLGmp$, i.e.\ a
  $1$-form modulo the action of the positive loop group.
  The \emph{polar part} of $f(t)dt \in k((t))dt$ is its class in
  $k((t))dt / k[[t]]dt \cong k[t^{-1}]t^{-1}dt$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S2.3.1.}

nodes.title: Logarithmic decomposition of $\LocSysGm$
nodes.statement:
There is a canonical map $\log : \LLGm \to \LLA$ that induces a presentation
  of $\LocSysGm$ via a Cartesian square involving the residue map
  $\Res : \LLAdt \to k$ (sending $f(t)dt \mapsto$ coefficient of $t^{-1}$)
  and the de Rham part $\on{Ker}(\Res)_{dR}$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S2.3.3.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:locsys-gm, def:polar-forms}
  The map $d\log : \LLGmp \to \LLAp dt$ and the evaluation-at-$0$ map
  $\on{ev} : \LLGmp \to \Gm$ combine to give an isomorphism
  $(\LLGmp \xrightarrow{(d\log,\,\on{ev})} \LLAp dt \times \Gm)$,
  which is verified on $A$-points: $(g = \sum a_i t^i) \mapsto
  (g^{-1}dg,\, a_0)$ is invertible by the formal power series inverse.
  The logarithmic local systems $\LocSysGm_{\log}$ are then identified
  with $\mathfrak{L}^{\mathrm{pol}}\BA^1 dt \times B\Gm$ via this
  isomorphism and the quotient construction.

nodes.title: Decomposition of $\LocSysGm$
nodes.statement:
There is an isomorphism of prestacks
  \[
    \LocSysGm \;\cong\; \BA^1/\BZ \;\times\; \on{Ker}(\Res)_{dR}
    \;\times\; B\Gm.
  \]
  Here $\BA^1/\BZ$ classifies the residue (a $k$-point plus a monodromy),
  $\on{Ker}(\Res)_{dR}$ is the de Rham local system with trivial residue
  (an ind-pro-scheme), and $B\Gm$ is the classifying stack of $\Gm$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Proposition 2.3.5.1.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{prop:locsys-log-decomp}
  From Proposition~\ref{prop:locsys-log-decomp}, the logarithmic local
  systems $\LocSysGm_{\log} \cong \mathfrak{L}^{\mathrm{pol}}\BA^1 dt
  \times B\Gm$ with residue decomposition
  $\mathfrak{L}^{\mathrm{pol}}\BA^1 dt = \BA^1 \times \on{Ker}(\Res)$.
  The Grassmannian $\on{Gr}_{\Gm}$ decomposes as $\BZ \times \on{Gr}_{\Gm}^{\circ}$
  via the (minus-) valuation map, where the $\BZ$-factor records the
  residue (contributing the $\BA^1/\BZ$ factor via the exponential) and
  $d\log$ identifies $\on{Gr}_{\Gm}^{\circ}$ with the formal group
  $\on{Ker}(\Res)_0^{\wedge}$. Quotienting by the $\LLGm$-action gives
  $\LocSysGm \cong \BA^1/\BZ \times \on{Ker}(\Res)_{dR} \times B\Gm$.

nodes.title: The prestack $\cY'$
nodes.statement:
Define the prestack
  \[
    \cY' \;:=\; \Eq\!\Big(\LLA \times \LLAdt
    \;\underset{(g,\omega)\mapsto g\omega}{\overset{d \circ \pi_1}{\rightrightarrows}}\;
    \LLAdt\Big) \;\in\; \PreStk.
  \]
  Concretely, the $A$-points of $\cY'$ are pairs $(f, \omega) \in A((t)) \times
  A((t))dt$ satisfying $df = \omega$, i.e.\ $\omega = f'(t)dt$; so $\cY'$
  encodes a function $f$ together with its de Rham differential.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Definition 2.4.1.1.}

nodes.title: The space $\cY$
nodes.statement:
The \emph{de Rham moduli space} is the stack quotient
  \[
    \cY \;:=\; \cY' / \LLGm
  \]
  where $\LLGm$ acts on $\cY'$ by $g \cdot (f,\omega) = (gf,\, g\omega)$.
  There is a canonical map $\pi : \cY \to \LocSysGm$ given by
  $(f,\omega) \mapsto \omega$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Definition 2.4.1.1.}

nodes.title: $\cY$ is classical
nodes.statement:
$\cY$ is a classical prestack. In particular, the derived equalizer
  defining $\cY'$ is concentrated in cohomological degree $0$: the natural map
  from the underived equalizer to $\cY'$ is an isomorphism.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Theorem 2.4.3.1.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:Y, def:Z-leq-n, lem:flat-multiplication}
  Each truncated space $\cZ^{\leq n}$ is a classical algebraic stack
  (Proposition~p:zn-cl in \cite{hilburn-raskin}): the map
  $\mu : \LLAp \times \LLAn{n}dt \to \LLAn{n}dt$ given by
  $(g,\omega) \mapsto g\omega - dg$ is flat by a consequence of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:flat-multiplication}, so its zero fiber
  $\mu^{-1}(0)$ is classical, and since this fiber is the universal
  $\LLGmp$-torsor over $\cZ^{\leq n}$, the prestack $\cZ^{\leq n}$
  is itself classical. The space $\cY = \varinjlim_n \cZ^{\leq n}$
  is then classical as a filtered colimit of classical prestacks.

nodes.title: Truncated spaces $\cZ^{\leq n}$
nodes.statement:
For $n \geq 1$, the \emph{$n$-th truncated moduli space} is the prestack
  \[
    \cZ^{\leq n} \;:=\; \Big(\cY' \underset{\LLAdt}{\times} \LLAn{n}dt\Big)
    \Big/ \LLGm.
  \]
  Concretely, $\cZ^{\leq n}$ parametrizes pairs $(f,\omega)$ where $f \in k((t))$
  and $\omega \in (k[[t]]/t^n)dt$, satisfying $df \equiv \omega \pmod{t^n dt}$.
  There are closed immersions $\cZ^{\leq n} \hookrightarrow \cZ^{\leq n+1}$
  and $\cY = \varinjlim_n \cZ^{\leq n}$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Definition 2.5.1.1.}

nodes.title: $\cZ^{\leq n}$ is an algebraic stack
nodes.statement:
For each $n \geq 1$, the prestack $\cZ^{\leq n}$ is a smooth algebraic stack
  locally of finite type over $k$. The map $\cZ^{\leq n} \to \LocSysGm$ is
  representable and faithfully flat.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Proposition 2.5.3.1.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:Z-leq-n, lem:flat-multiplication, prop:locsys-log-decomp}
  From the definition of $\cZ^{\leq n}$ as a fiber product, the equalizer
  formula shows that the map $\cZ^{\leq n} \to \LocSysGm_{\log}^{\leq n}$
  is affine. The truncated logarithmic local systems
  $\LocSysGm_{\log}^{\leq n}$ form an algebraic stack
  (Proposition~p:locsys-trun in \cite{hilburn-raskin}, which follows from
  Proposition~\ref{prop:locsys-log-decomp} and the algebraicity of
  $\LLAn{n}$). A morphism that is affine over an algebraic stack remains
  algebraic, so $\cZ^{\leq n}$ is an algebraic stack.
  Smoothness and local finite type follow from the flatness of $\mu_n$
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat-multiplication}) and the explicit local description
  of $\cZ^{\leq n}$ in coordinates.

nodes.title: Truncated convolution map $\mu_n$
nodes.statement:
The \emph{truncated convolution map} $\mu_n : \BA^n \times \BA^n \to \BA^n$
  corresponds to multiplication of truncated power series:
  \[
    \mu_n\bigl((a_0,\ldots,a_{n-1}),\,(b_0,\ldots,b_{n-1})\bigr)
    \;:=\; \Big(\sum_{i+j=0} a_i b_j,\; \sum_{i+j=1} a_i b_j,\; \ldots,\;
    \sum_{i+j=n-1} a_i b_j\Big).
  \]
  This is the scheme map $\LLAn{n} \times_k \LLAn{n} \to \LLAn{n}$ induced by
  multiplication $k[[t]]/t^n \otimes_k k[[t]]/t^n \to k[[t]]/t^n$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S2.6.1.}

nodes.title: Flatness of truncated convolution
nodes.statement:
The truncated convolution map $\mu_n : \BA^{2n} \to \BA^n$ is flat.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Lemma 2.6.1.1.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:trunc-conv}
  The map $\mu_n$ is defined by degree-$2$ homogeneous polynomials, so
  by a standard homogeneity argument it suffices to show that the central
  fiber $Z := \mu_n^{-1}(0) \subset \BA^{2n}$ is equidimensional of
  dimension $n$. Stratify $Z$ by locally closed subschemes
  $Z_m$ ($0 \leq m \leq n$) where $a_0 = \cdots = a_{m-1} = 0$ and
  $a_m \neq 0$ (vacuous for $m = n$). For $m < n$, the equations
  $\sum_{i=0}^j a_{m+i} b_{j-i} = 0$ ($j = 0,\ldots,n-m-1$) allow one
  to express $b_0,\ldots,b_{n-m-1}$ uniquely in terms of $a_m,\ldots,a_{n-1}$
  and $b_{n-m},\ldots,b_{n-1}$, giving an isomorphism
  $Z_m \cong (\BA^1 \setminus 0) \times \BA^{n-m-1} \times \BA^m$,
  which has dimension $n$. Hence $Z$ is equidimensional of dimension $n$,
  so $\mu_n$ is flat.

nodes.title: Coherent sheaves on an eventually coconnective affine
nodes.statement:
Let $S$ be an eventually coconnective affine derived scheme (i.e.\ $\mathcal{O}(S)$
  is connective and $H^i(\mathcal{O}(S)) = 0$ for $i \gg 0$).
  The full DG subcategory
  \[
    \CohS(S) \;\subset\; \QCohS(S)
  \]
  consists of those quasi-coherent sheaves whose cohomology sheaves are coherent
  $H^0(\mathcal{O}(S))$-modules that are bounded (lie in finitely many degrees).

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S4.1.1.}

nodes.title: Ind-coherent sheaves $\IndCohStar(S)$
nodes.statement:
For an eventually coconnective affine derived scheme $S$, define
  \[
    \IndCohStar(S) \;:=\; \Ind(\CohS(S)),
  \]
  the ind-completion of $\CohS(S)$. There is a canonical functor
  $\Psi : \IndCohStar(S) \to \QCohS(S)$ extending the inclusion
  $\CohS(S) \hookrightarrow \QCohS(S)$ by colimits.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S4.2.1.}

nodes.title: $\Psi$ is an equivalence on bounded-below objects
nodes.statement:
The functor $\Psi : \IndCohStar(S) \to \QCohS(S)$ is $t$-exact and induces
  an equivalence
  \[
    \Psi : \IndCohStar(S)^+ \;\simeq\; \QCohS(S)^+
  \]
  on the bounded-below (eventually coconnective) full subcategories.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Lemma 4.2.1.2, citing \cite{gaitsgory-rozenblyum-methods}, Lemma 6.4.1.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:indcoh-star-S}
  This is Lemma~6.4.1 of Gaitsgory--Rozenblyum \cite{gaitsgory-rozenblyum-methods}.
  $t$-exactness of $\Psi$ follows because $\Psi$ is the ind-extension of
  the $t$-exact inclusion $\CohS(S) \hookrightarrow \QCohS(S)$.
  For essential surjectivity on bounded-below objects: given
  $M \in \QCohS(S)^+$, write $M = \varinjlim_r \tau^{\geq -r} M$.
  Each $\tau^{\geq -r} M$ is bounded below, and by the eventually
  coconnective hypothesis on $S$, every bounded-below quasi-coherent
  sheaf is a filtered colimit of objects in $\CohS(S)$ (using
  truncation $\tau^{\geq -r}$ and the fact that $\CohS(S)$ generates
  $\QCohS(S)^+$). Hence $M \in \Psi(\IndCohStar(S)^+)$, giving
  essential surjectivity. Full faithfulness on bounded-below objects
  follows from the universal property of $\Ind(\CohS(S))$.

nodes.title: t-structure on $\IndCohStar(S)$
nodes.statement:
There is a unique $t$-structure on $\IndCohStar(S)$ such that $\Psi$ is
  $t$-exact. Under this $t$-structure, the heart
  $\IndCohStar(S)^{\heartsuit} \simeq \on{QCoh}(H^0(\mathcal{O}(S)))\mbox{-}\on{mod}^{coh}$
  is the abelian category of coherent $H^0(\mathcal{O}(S))$-modules.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S4.2.2.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:indcoh-star-S, lem:psi-equiv}
  Existence: define $\IndCohStar(S)^{\leq 0}$ to be the preimage of
  $\QCohS(S)^{\leq 0}$ under $\Psi$. Since $\Psi$ is $t$-exact
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:psi-equiv}), it intertwines the truncation functors
  $\tau^{\leq 0}$ on $\IndCohStar(S)$ and $\QCohS(S)$, and the axioms
  of a $t$-structure (existence of left and right truncations, the
  half-plane conditions) follow from those on $\QCohS(S)$ by transport
  along $\Psi$. Uniqueness is clear since $\Psi$ is $t$-exact and
  essentially surjective on bounded-below objects (Lemma~\ref{lem:psi-equiv}).
  The identification of the heart with coherent $H^0(\mathcal{O}(S))$-modules
  follows from the heart of the standard $t$-structure on $\QCohS(S)$.

nodes.title: Semi-coherent algebra
Semi-coherence of polynomial algebras
nodes.statement:
The coordinate ring $A_n := \mathcal{O}(\LLAn{n}) \cong k[x_0,\ldots,x_{n-1}]$
  is semi-coherent. Since $A_n$ is a finitely generated polynomial algebra over
  a field (hence Noetherian), every finitely generated module is coherent,
  which implies semi-coherence.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S4.3.2.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:truncated-positive-loop-space, def:semi-coherent}
  The coordinate ring $A_n = k[x_0,\ldots,x_{n-1}]$ is a finitely generated
  polynomial algebra over a field, hence Noetherian. By the example following
  the definition of semi-coherence in \cite{hilburn-raskin} §4.3.1, any
  Noetherian (and eventually coconnective) commutative algebra is
  semi-coherent: every bounded-below module is a filtered colimit of its
  finitely generated $A_n$-submodules, each of which is coherent (by
  Noetherianity) and lives in a uniformly bounded range of cohomological
  degrees (by the bounded-below hypothesis).

nodes.title: $\IndCohStar(\cY)$ via Grassmannian weak-invariants
nodes.statement:
Since $\cY = \varinjlim_n \cZ^{\leq n}$ is an ind-algebraic stack, define
  \[
    \IndCohStar(\cY) \;:=\; \varprojlim_n \IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})
  \]
  as the limit along the $*$-pullback maps
  $\IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n+1}) \to \IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S4.4.1.}

nodes.title: Full faithfulness of $\lambda_n^*$
nodes.statement:
For each $n \geq 1$, the restriction functor
  \[
    \lambda_n^* : \IndCohStar(\cY) \;\longrightarrow\; \IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})
  \]
  induced by the closed immersion $\lambda_n : \cZ^{\leq n} \hookrightarrow \cY$
  is fully faithful on objects supported on $\cZ^{\leq n}$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S4.4.2.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:indcoh-star-Y, def:Z-leq-n, prop:locsys-log-decomp}
  The closed immersion $\lambda_n : \cZ^{\leq n} \hookrightarrow \cY$ is
  formally étale for $n > 0$: this follows from the formally étale property
  of the truncated LocSys comparison
  $\LocSysGm^{\leq n} \to \LocSysGm$ (Proposition~\ref{prop:locsys-log-decomp}).
  For an object $\mathcal{F}$ supported on $\cZ^{\leq n}$, the counit
  $\lambda_{n,*}^{\IndCoh} \circ \lambda_n^* \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{F}$
  is an isomorphism because the formal étaleness implies the pushforward-pullback
  composition is the identity on the subcategory of objects supported on
  $\cZ^{\leq n}$. Full faithfulness of $\lambda_n^*$ on this subcategory
  then follows from the unit-counit adjunction.

nodes.title: Weyl algebra $W_n$
D-module category on the loop space
nodes.statement:
The \emph{D-module category on the loop space} is the colimit
  \[
    D^!(\LLA) \;:=\; \varinjlim_n\; D(\LLAn{n})
    \;\simeq\; \varinjlim_n\; W_n\mbox{-}\on{mod},
  \]
  taken along the transition functors $D(\LLAn{n}) \to D(\LLAn{n+1})$ induced
  by the closed embeddings $\LLAn{n} \hookrightarrow \LLAn{n+1}$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S5.1.2.}

nodes.title: Spectral realization
nodes.statement:
For each $n \geq 1$, there exists a canonical object
  $\cF_n \in \IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})$ and an algebra isomorphism
  \[
    W_n^{op} \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
    \underline{\on{End}}_{\IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})}(\cF_n).
  \]
  These objects $\{\cF_n\}$ are compatible under the transition maps
  $\lambda_n^*$, yielding a canonical object $\cF \in \IndCohStar(\cY)$.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, \S5.3.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:weyl-algebra-Wn, def:indcoh-star-Y, def:Z-leq-n, lem:lambda-n-ff,
        prop:Z-leq-n-algebraic, prop:indcoh-t-structure}
  Define $\cF_n := \on{Av}_!^{\on{Gr}_{\Gm}^{\leq n}, w}
  (i_{n,*}(\mathcal{O}_{\cZ^{\leq n}})) \in \IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})$,
  the weak Grassmannian averaging of the pushforward of the structure sheaf
  along the embedding $i_n : \cZ^{\leq n} \hookrightarrow \cY_{\log}^{\leq n}$.
  This object lies in the heart of the $t$-structure and is compact.
  An algebra homomorphism $W_n^{op} \to \underline{\on{End}}_{\IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})}(\cF_n)$
  is constructed by specifying generators: position operators $\varphi_\omega$
  (for $\omega \in (k[[t]]/t^n)^\vee$) and momentum operators $\theta_f$
  (for $f \in k[[t]]/t^n$), and verifying the Weyl commutation relations.
  The generators act geometrically via the correspondence between $\cZ^{\leq n}$
  and the flat structure on $\LLAn{n}$.
  That this map is an isomorphism (the key content of the theorem) is
  Proposition~p:ff in \cite{hilburn-raskin}.
  Compatibility of the $\{\cF_n\}$ under $\lambda_n^*$
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:lambda-n-ff}) yields $\cF \in \IndCohStar(\cY)$.

nodes.title: Main functor is fully faithful
nodes.statement:
The functor
  \[
    \Delta \;:=\; \varinjlim_n \Delta_n \;:\; D^!(\LLA) \;\longrightarrow\;
    \IndCohStar(\cY)
  \]
  constructed via the spectral realization (Theorem~\ref{thm:spectral-realization})
  is fully faithful.

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Theorem 7.0.0.1.}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:spectral-realization, prop:indcoh-t-structure, lem:lambda-n-ff,
        def:dmod-loop-space}
  The argument proceeds by induction on $n$. At level $n$, the functor
  $\Delta_n : D(\LLAn{n}) \simeq W_n\mbox{-}\on{mod} \to \IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})$
  is fully faithful (Proposition~7.0.0.1 in \cite{hilburn-raskin}, proved via
  the spectral realization of Theorem~\ref{thm:spectral-realization}): for any
  $M, N \in W_n\mbox{-}\on{mod}$, the map
  $\Hom_{W_n}(M,N) \to \Hom_{\IndCohStar(\cZ^{\leq n})}(\Delta_n M, \Delta_n N)$
  is an isomorphism. The base case $n = 0$ is checked by explicit
  calculation; the inductive step uses the local geometric class field theory
  pairing (Contou-Carrère) to compare Hom spaces at level $n+1$ with those
  at level $n$. Full faithfulness of $\Delta = \varinjlim_n \Delta_n$ then
  follows by passing to the colimit, using that a filtered colimit of
  fully faithful functors between compactly generated categories remains
  fully faithful (Lemma~l:colim-ff in \cite{hilburn-raskin}).

nodes.title: Main equivalence
nodes.statement:
The functor $\Delta : D^!(\LLA) \to \IndCohStar(\cY)$ is an equivalence of
  DG categories:
  \[
    \Delta \;:\; D^!(\LLA) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\; \IndCohStar(\cY).
  \]
  This is the de Rham analogue of Tate's theorem in local class field theory:
  a canonical equivalence compatible with the local geometric class field theory
  action of Beilinson--Drinfeld. Essential surjectivity follows from the
  exhaustion of $\cY$ by $\cZ^{\leq n}$ combined with t-structure estimates,
  together with full faithfulness
  (Theorem~\ref{thm:fully-faithful}).

  \textit{Source: \cite{hilburn-raskin}, Theorem 8.4.0.1 (= Theorem 1.1.0.1).}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:fully-faithful, thm:Y-classical, prop:Z-leq-n-algebraic,
        prop:An-semi-coherent, thm:spectral-realization, def:indcoh-star-Y}
  Full faithfulness of $\Delta$ is established in
  Theorem~\ref{thm:fully-faithful}. For essential surjectivity, observe
  that the essential image of $\Delta$ contains all objects of the form
  $\lambda_{n,*}^{\IndCoh}(\mathcal{O}_{\cZ^{\leq n}}(m))$ for $n \geq 0$,
  $m \in \mathbb{Z}$, arising from line bundles on the truncated spaces
  $\cZ^{\leq n}$ (which are algebraic by
  Proposition~\ref{prop:Z-leq-n-algebraic}).
  The classicality of $\cY$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Y-classical}) and the
  semi-coherence of the coordinate rings (Proposition~\ref{prop:An-semi-coherent})
  together imply that $\IndCohStar(\cY)$ is generated under filtered colimits
  by these objects (via Corollary c:z-gen in \cite{hilburn-raskin}).
  The generation result, combined with the spectral realization of $W_n^{op}$
  (Theorem~\ref{thm:spectral-realization}), shows every object of
  $\IndCohStar(\cY)$ lies in the image of $\Delta$.
